title: SPARQL PrefixedNames containing 2 colons don't parse
description:
DBpedia endpoint:
prefix ns1: <http://dbpedia.org/page/>
select * { ?s ?p ns1:Category:Software_bugs }

On closer inspection ns1:Category:Software_bugs is however both: a valid CURIE and a valid SPARQL PrefixedName, so this is likely a Virtuoso bug.
re RDFLib/rdflib#867
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, category VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, category) VALUES (1, 'Software Bug', 'ns1:Category:Software_bugs');
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE category IS NOT NULL;
UPDATE test_table SET category = NULL WHERE id = 1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE category LIKE 'ns1:%';
key_bug_features: SPARQL PrefixedNames with two colons fail to parse
